title: EFT: Case Formulation
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides for case formulation in EFT.

sources.label: Goldman and Goldstein, Case formulation in emotion-focused therapy

slides.title: Marker: the pattern needs a map
slides.body: Use formulation to orient task choice while preserving discovery.
bullets: Repeated emotional sequences appear across stories.
Secondary emotion obscures primary pain, need, or action.
Client and therapist need a shared next-step map.

slides.title: Build the map from live process
slides.body: Formulate from what the client does emotionally in session, not only from what they report about life.
steps: Track presenting problem, current triggers, and repeated interpersonal context.
Identify emotion processing style: access, avoidance, interruption, escalation, collapse.
Differentiate secondary, primary maladaptive, and primary adaptive emotion.
Name the core pain, unmet need, and protective strategy in ordinary language.
Link markers to tasks: focusing, unfolding, chair work, soothing, alliance work.
Offer the formulation as a testable collaboration: is this close, or not quite?

slides.title: Use formulation to choose the next task
